concept: The Launch Call — a rural Alaska medevac decision, made visible: a roadless village on a map, a clinic worker keying a radio into worsening weather, an instrument panel that weighs vitals + weather + aircraft into one rising risk score, and the honest turn: a hand physically stops the machine and hand-drafts the still-unwritten rule for who owns that data and who consents to it. The story ends where it must: a person's hand, not the model, still makes the call.
references: Stripe/Linear-style clean instrument-panel product design (calm, confident data surfaces, no glossy sci-fi HUD glow)
Ken Burns-style restrained documentary pacing (measured holds, no forced excitement, respecting the human stakes)

taste_dials.notes: This is a quiet, deliberate story, not a race or a countdown, so motion intensity sits low (4) against last week's breathing tide (5) and the week before's ten-minute clock (5). Design variance is high (7): the fullbleed-split ledger shot and the rising-row-of-hands shot are genuinely asymmetric departures from the console/map shots. Texture grain stays low (4), cleaner and more clinical than the last two dispatches' filmic ocean/ember grain, because this is a contemporary policy-and-medicine story, not a natural landscape.

palette: overcast gunmetal-grey sky over pale birch-white snow, a warm marigold-orange risk needle, a cool glacier-blue console glow, and one flat charcoal void for the still-unwritten rule
metaphor: the model can score the risk before the plane leaves the ground. The rulebook for who owns the data hasn't been written yet, and a person's hand, not the model, still makes the call
honest_caveat: This is a community-engaged governance framework plus an early pilot research model (ANTHC, Southcentral Foundation, Maniilaq, UAF CANHR, UAA, and Stanford, published in Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence and the International Journal of Circumpolar Health), not a deployed clinical tool. No clinic runs it today. The peer-reviewed record itself says no ethical guidelines yet exist for AI or ML in the Alaska Native and American Indian tribal health systems. Drawn on screen as a PHYSICAL action, a hand stops the climbing gauge and hand-drafts a dotted, dissolving guardrail line that never fully resolves, so a muted viewer reads the hedge without a single word.
voice: kokoro:am_michael (warm, trustworthy, explainer/field-report tone) — publish; avoids repeating the last 3 dispatches' voices (af_heart, am_fenrir, bf_emma).
music_mood: restrained, tender, a slow held tension that resolves warm, not oceanic-ambient (already used) and not dark/tense-electronic (already used).

fingerprint.palette: overcast gunmetal-grey sky over pale birch-white snow, a warm marigold-orange risk needle, a cool glacier-blue console glow, and one flat charcoal void for the still-unwritten rule
fingerprint.metaphor: the model can score the risk before the plane leaves the ground. The rulebook for who owns the data hasn't been written yet, and a person's hand, not the model, still makes the call

divergence_note: Unlike 07-03's frantic orbital HUD countdown swarming over a wildfire canopy (radial-emanate, multiplicity-swarm, instrument-readout) and 07-05's calm vertical cross-section descent through an ocean water column (scroll-data, instrument-as-subject, stacked-layers, editorial-schematic), this piece stays mostly earthbound and deliberately restrained: a top-down village map, an interior human hand on a radio, a booting instrument panel, a paper ledger split-frame where a hand physically halts the machine, and an eye-level rising row of hands placing named plates, cut together with builds, match-cuts, and a graphic-match rather than radial scans or vertical scrolling, rendered in a clean blueprint-technical register neither of the last two dispatches used, and closing on a human hand pressing a physical launch switch rather than a machine readout. (Revised after Gate 0B: the community beat was originally an orbital-aerial ring of light points, which read as a relabeled copy of 07-03's entire orbital-aerial/radial-emanate/multiplicity-swarm identity; it is now an eye-level, grid-modular, vertical-rise row of hands and plates, sharing no more than one axis with any prior dispatch's full fingerprint.)
timing_note: Beat/shot times below are ANCHORED TO THE REAL synthesized VO (.claude/skills/alaska-dispatch/audio/timing60.json + words60.json), not hand-estimated seconds. Kokoro am_michael at speed=1.14 ends speech at 55.68s (a trimmed ~128-word script; the first 143-word draft ran to ~67s with per-clip gaps, too long for a 60s master). The single planned beat 9 ('into a single score, trained on...') was split into two synthesis calls (new beats 9 and 10) because as one clip it created a 5.4s gap to the next beat, over the 5.0s never-rest ceiling. The shot2/shot3 boundary also moved one beat later (the fui-boot transition now lands at the start of 'is testing a model built for that call.' instead of 'Alaska's tribal health system, working with Stanford,') because shot3 as originally planned would have run 17.2s, over the 16.0s max-shot-seconds ceiling; letting the clinic scene hold one beat longer fixed it without touching the ceiling.

shots.centered: a single marigold pin already pulsing alone in the black void at frame 1, faint weather static closing in around it, before the rest of the dark, roadless map of Alaska fades up around it
shots.means: hardens the cold open: the stake lands at frame 1, not after a slow fade, a muted scroll stops here

shots.centered: the health aide's hand keying the radio, monitor glow lighting their face, snow thickening at the window
shots.means: travels from the abstract map into the specific human moment it represents

shots.centered: the converging risk gauge needle climbing as three data channels (vitals, weather, aircraft) lock into place
shots.means: the human call becomes the model's inputs, booting into being out of the radio static

shots.centered: a hand entering frame to physically stop the climbing gauge needle, then the same hand drawing a dotted, unresolved guardrail line across a nearly blank ledger page
shots.means: carries the gauge's glow into the honest limit as a PHYSICAL action, legible muted, not a passive text stamp

shots.centered: hands placing named plates, ANTHC, Southcentral, Maniilaq, community, one by one into a rising horizontal row, each locking into its slot
shots.means: the dissolving guardrail line resolves into who is actually doing the writing, hands, not an abstract swarm, no invented imagery

shots.centered: a plane icon completing its crossing to the village pin as a human thumb physically presses a large tactile launch switch beside it, then the branded outro
shots.means: bookends the map, shows a person's physical hand, not the model, making the final call, then closes on the wordmark

beats.vo: Eighty percent of Alaska's communities have no road out.
beats.shows: COLD OPEN: at frame 1 a single marigold pin is already pulsing alone in black void, faint weather static closing in around it; only after impact does the rest of the dark, roadless map of Alaska fade up around it
beats.sfx: a low ambient wind swell arriving with the pin's first pulse, a soft rising pad
beats.means: hardens the hook at frame 1, not after a slow fade, a muted scroll stops here

beats.vo: When someone in a village is critically sick,
beats.shows: The camera pushes slowly toward the pulsing pin as more of the roadless map resolves into view around it
beats.sfx: the pulse continues, a heartbeat-like thump
beats.means: the abstract map becomes personal: one place, implicitly one person

beats.vo: the only way out is by air,
beats.shows: Map-travel: a small plane silhouette flashes briefly across the distant sky as the glow becomes a window's light; the frame arrives inside a clinic room at night
beats.sfx: a whoosh as the world changes, a faint distant engine drone under the wind fading to an interior hush
beats.means: first shot change: travels from the map into the human world it represents, with a literal, if brief, image of by air

beats.vo: and a clinic worker faces the hardest call in rural medicine:
beats.shows: A health aide's hand reaches into frame for a radio handset; a patient monitor's glow lights their face
beats.means: grounds the stake in one specific human gesture, held center-frame

beats.shows: The aide keys the radio. Through the window behind them, blowing snow visibly thickens
beats.sfx: a radio key-click, a gust of wind against glass
beats.means: the decision's real cost made visible: the weather is worsening as they decide

beats.vo: Alaska's tribal health system, working with Stanford,
beats.shows: Still inside the clinic: the aide holds the radio steady as a dispatch voice relays the tribal health system's name; the window's storm light flickers
beats.sfx: a radio static murmur, a distant thunder-like rumble
beats.means: lets the human moment breathe one beat longer before the world changes

beats.vo: is testing a model built for that call.
beats.shows: Fui-boot: the radio static resolves into an instrument panel booting into being, brackets snapping into frame
beats.sfx: a build arpeggio, brackets snapping in
beats.means: second shot change: the human moment becomes the model's world, timed to is testing a model

beats.vo: It weighs patient vitals, weather, and aircraft status
beats.shows: Three labeled channels appear in sequence, VITALS, WEATHER, AIRCRAFT, then each fills with a live readout: a heart-rate line, a radar sweep, a plane-status icon
beats.sfx: three ticks, one per channel, a soft data-hum layering in
beats.means: names and shows the model's real, unglamorous inputs concretely

beats.shows: The three channels visibly converge toward one point as a gauge begins forming
beats.sfx: a rising riser tone beginning to build
beats.means: the convergence begins: many inputs start collapsing into one

beats.vo: trained on years of real flights across the tribal health system.
beats.shows: The converged gauge's needle climbs to its final position; a small readout reads 'YEARS OF FLIGHT DATA'
beats.sfx: the riser peaks and resolves as the needle settles
beats.means: the mechanism's payoff completes: many inputs collapse into one number

beats.shows: Match-cut: the gauge's glow becomes the edge-light on a facing page; a hand enters frame and physically stops the gauge's needle mid-climb
beats.sfx: a designed near-silence, one low tone dropping out, a soft mechanical stop-click
beats.means: third shot change and the honest caveat begins as a physical action, a hand halts the machine, not a caption

beats.vo: It's a research pilot. The rules for who owns that data,
beats.shows: The same hand begins drawing a dotted, unresolved guardrail line across the nearly blank ledger page
beats.means: the hedge staged as a hand-drawn, tentative line, not a solid claim, legible with the sound off

beats.vo: and who consents to it, still haven't been written.
beats.shows: The dotted line keeps dissolving at its edges as the hand pulls back, refusing to fully resolve into a clean line
beats.sfx: a faint paper-rustle held under a long low tone
beats.means: commits visually to unresolved, not a tidy answer

beats.vo: The community writes the guardrails before the model touches a patient.
beats.shows: Graphic-match: hands enter and finish drawing the machine's still-dissolving line into a solid baseline, then place named plates (ANTHC, Southcentral, Maniilaq) onto it one by one
beats.sfx: a soft mechanical click per plate locking into place
beats.means: fourth shot change: hands physically COMPLETE what the machine's line could not, legible muted as people finishing the unwritten rule

beats.vo: The score can inform the call.
beats.shows: A final plate reading 'community' locks in beside the others; the risk gauge reappears in miniature at the end of the row, its needle held steady beside the hands, visibly advisory, not commanding
beats.sfx: a warm resolve tone, a soft final click
beats.means: draws the score's role explicitly as advisory, sitting beside the human row, not above it

beats.vo: The person on the radio still makes it.
beats.shows: Whip-pan: fifth shot change, back to the map; the plane icon completes its crossing to the glowing pin as a human thumb physically presses a large tactile launch switch beside it
beats.sfx: a whip-pan whoosh into an engine hum, then a solid mechanical switch-press thunk
beats.means: the thesis lands in one motion: word and payoff image lock together, a person's hand, not the model, makes the final call

beats.shows: The switch's glow resolves into a clean light-sweep as the ALASKA.AI wordmark begins to fade up center-frame
beats.means: the brand beat rides the resolved thesis rather than carrying new plot weight

beats.shows: Staged outro: tagline and sources credit stamp in beneath the wordmark in sequence, then a slow cinematic fade while the plane's engine hum tapers
beats.sfx: a final resolve chord, engine hum tapering to quiet
beats.means: deliberate branded ending; motion keeps running (fade plus tapering hum) to the final frame, never a static hold
